rateOutlook.source: CME FedWatch (as of 2026-03-27). Iran war escalation and oil at $112 have some traders pricing in hike possibility. Core PCE 2.7% supports cuts, but energy inflation is a wildcard

events.name: ISM Manufacturing PMI
watchPoints: Impact of March oil price surge ($112) on manufacturing costs
Prices subindex (70.5 last month) — further rise would intensify inflation fears
Supply chain disruption risk from Iran war

events.name: Retail Sales (February data)
watchPoints: February data delayed to April 1 release
Impact of rising energy costs on consumer spending
Consumer pullback following employment shock (-92K)

events.name: Non-Farm Payrolls (NFP)
watchPoints: Rebound from February's -92K shock is critical
Healthcare strike resolution, federal government further cuts
Unemployment rate: holding at 4.1% or rising
April 3 is Good Friday — early market close

events.name: FOMC Minutes (March 17-18 Meeting)
watchPoints: Context for Powell's 'inflation not coming down as expected' remark
Which members shifted dot plot projections toward fewer cuts
Committee views on Iran war/oil inflation impact
Assessment of employment slowdown (-92K)

events.name: Consumer Price Index (CPI)
watchPoints: First month to capture March oil price surge ($112) in energy CPI
Whether core CPI continues declining from 2.5%
Shelter cost deceleration pace is key
PPI +0.7% consumer pass-through

events.name: Producer Price Index (PPI)
watchPoints: March energy price surge fully reflected in PPI
Another above-expected reading would confirm inflation re-acceleration
Tariff impacts on goods pricing persistence

events.name: FOMC Meeting
watchPoints: Inflation outlook amid prolonged Iran war and $112 oil
Core PCE 2.7% below target vs rising energy inflation — conflicting signals
Dot plot update (if available)
Powell press conference tone — hawkish hold or dovish pivot
If employment weakness deepens, possible rate cut signaling

events.name: GDP (Q1 2026 Advance Estimate)
watchPoints: Q1 included employment shock (-92K), oil surge, and Iran war
Consumer spending slowdown reflection in GDP
Net exports possibly worsening from oil price surge
Reference Atlanta Fed GDPNow tracker

events.name: Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE)
watchPoints: The Fed's preferred inflation measure
$112 oil energy costs beginning to flow into March PCE
Core PCE bounce from 2.7% would eliminate rate cut expectations
RSMus 'calm before the storm' analysis — energy pass-through is key
